gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 found that matrix target coercion still referenced the mutable public document target class; the wrapper now checks stable target bases and a regression test covers valid contaminated matrix targets plus invalid object rejection. Final re-review reported no findings.
verification: GPT-5.5 review completed; P1 target coercion finding fixed and re-review approved
what_changed: Loaded pristine document_kv_cache.databricks_engine_probe_job defaults from source for the legacy restaurant_kv_serving compatibility wrapper.
Moved engine-probe legacy config classes and constants onto source-default fallbacks when public document symbols are replaced before import.
Kept matrix probe target coercion on stable legacy/source bases instead of the mutable public document class.
why: The legacy package boundary should remain behavior-compatible even when tests or downstream integrations monkeypatch the new document namespace before importing the old restaurant namespace.
